body_txt: World cultures tend to have expectations of what is appropriate behavior for men and women. This naturally influences the works of fiction that come out of those cultures. This trope relates to how cultural expectations shape the audience's perception of a female character. If Alice doesn't have any of the typical traits, goals, behaviors, emotions, etc. common to the cultural image of 'feminine,' she may be criticized for being too masculine, too unappealing , or too unsympathetic. Conversely, if she does not break from the stereotypically feminine at all, she may come under fire from the other side, which won't take Alice seriously if she is too 'girly' . Similarly, when Bob has these traits or goals, he may seem unmanly, unless the work makes a point of emphasizing that Real Men Wear Pink . Note that while this can be gender stereotyping ( which is a bad thing ) it can also be a way of rounding out a character . Even Action Girls or Wrench Wenches can have interests and talents besides kicking ass or fixing machines. A strong female character isn't any less strong because she can make a pot roast like nobody's business . See also Acceptable Masculine Goals and Traits .
